title: RENTING A CAR
conversation: Peter talks to Sam at the rental car counter about renting a car. Sam tells Peter about a special offer they're having and helps him understand how the rental fees work. Sam: Hello. How can I help you? Peter: I'd like to rent a car for the weekend. Sam: What size do you have in mind? Peter: I'd like your cheapest car, so I guess that would be a compact. Sam: We're running a special right now. You can rent a mid-size car for the same price as a compact. It's $55 a day inclusive of tax, plus insurance. It's such a great deal! Peter: Okay, I'll take it. Sam: All right. Let me just print out the agreement. . . Here you go, read that over, please. Peter: I can't make heads or tails of this information. Do I really need insurance? I already have a good auto insurance plan. Sam: Then you're probably all set. I do recommend the collision damage waiver.* It's only $10 a day. That way, if you bang up the car, you won't owe us anything. Peter: I'm sure I won't have an accident, but I'll take it just in case. Sam: Very good. If you don't return the car with a full tank of gas, we charge $9 a gallon to refill it. Peter: Nine bucks a gallon? That's so expensive. It's highway robbery! Sam: Be sure to return the car by 5 p.m. on Sunday. Peter: Five? That's going to be cutting it close. The conference I'm attending ends at 4:30. What if I get here at 6 on Sunday? Sam: It'll be another $65. Peter: There's no grace period? Sam: There's a 29 minute grace period. So, you could return the car at 5:29 and still be okay. Peter: Okay, thanks. You've been such a big help. Sam: You're very welcome. Here's the key. The car is in space A4. You're good to go.

idioms.meaning_en: to be thinking of something or have a plan/idea
idioms.meaning_vi: đang suy nghĩ về cái gì đó/đang cân nhắc
idioms.example: What kind of color do you have in mind for the bedroom?
idioms.context: What size do you have in mind?

idioms.meaning_en: offering a lower price or a special deal for a limited time
idioms.meaning_vi: đang có chương trình khuyến mãi đặc biệt
idioms.example: The grocery store is running a special on strawberries this week.
idioms.context: We're running a special right now.

idioms.example: The price of the hotel room is inclusive of breakfast.
idioms.context: It's $55 a day inclusive of tax, plus insurance.

idioms.meaning_en: to read something carefully from beginning to end
idioms.example: You should read the contract over before you sign it.
idioms.context: Here you go, read that over, please.

idioms.idiom: can't make heads or tails of
idioms.meaning_en: to be unable to understand something at all
idioms.meaning_vi: không hiểu đầu đuôi tai nheo gì cả/không hiểu chút nào
idioms.example: I can't make heads or tails of these assembly instructions.
idioms.context: I can't make heads or tails of this information.

idioms.meaning_vi: đã chuẩn bị xong/sẵn sàng/xong xuôi
idioms.example: We are all set for our trip to Japan.

idioms.meaning_en: to damage something (especially a vehicle) in a minor accident
idioms.meaning_vi: làm hư hỏng, va quẹt (thường là xe cộ)
idioms.example: He banged up his car when he hit a pole in the parking lot.
idioms.context: That way, if you bang up the car, you won't owe us anything.

idioms.meaning_en: to be prepared for a possible future event
idioms.example: Take an umbrella just in case it rains.
idioms.context: I'm sure I won't have an accident, but I'll take it just in case.

idioms.meaning_en: an extremely high price for something
idioms.meaning_vi: giá quá đắt/đắt cắt cổ (như là ăn cướp)
idioms.example: Charging $10 for a bottle of water is highway robbery.

idioms.meaning_en: to allow very little time for something to happen
idioms.example: We're cutting it close; the flight leaves in 30 minutes and we're still in traffic.
idioms.context: That's going to be cutting it close.

idioms.meaning_en: extra time allowed to do something without penalty
idioms.meaning_vi: thời gian ân hạn/thời gian gia hạn thêm
idioms.example: There is a 10-day grace period for paying your rent.

idioms.meaning_en: ready to leave or start something
idioms.meaning_vi: sẵn sàng để bắt đầu hoặc đi
idioms.example: Once you sign these papers, you're good to go.
